Benchmark Results for gpt-5.3-codex-MaxReasoning

Overall Summary

Test 1: Travelling Salesman Problem Solver (C++) Score: 0.00 / 21
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Travelling Salesman Problem (TSP) in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY number of cities from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 10-40 cities (brute-force feasible for smallest cases)
- **Medium**: 100-1000 cities (requires heuristics)
- **Large**: 5000-100000 cities (requires efficient heuristics)
- **Extreme**: 500000-5000000 cities (requires very fast approximations)
- **Epic**: 10000000+ cities (requires highly optimized approximations)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with city counts ranging from 10 to 10000000. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of cities)
Lines 2..N+1: x y coordinates (floats)

**Output format (stdout):**
One line with N integers: the visit order of city indices (0-indexed), starting at city 0.
Do NOT include returning to city 0 (return is implicit).

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for 1000,000,000 cities
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable routes at all scales
4. **Correctness**: Output must be a valid permutation of 0..N-1 starting with 0

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function that reads stdin and writes stdout.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on N.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: 10 Cities
View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a list of cities, find the shortest round-trip route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. This is one of the most famous hard problems in computer science — checking every possible route is only feasible for a handful of cities, so the AI must use clever shortcuts to find a good route quickly.

Subpasses scale from 10 cities up to 1 000, so brute-force is impossible for the larger cases. The AI's route length is compared against a simple baseline; beating it earns full marks.

 0.0000
[10 cities]

TSP Solver - 10 Cities

View Code (9717 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstdio>
#include <limits>
#include <numeric>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

class FastInput {
public:
    FastInput() : pos_(0), len_(0) {}

    bool readInt(int& out) {
        char c = nextNonSpace();
        if (!c) return false;

        int sign = 1;
        if (c == '-') {
            sign = -1;
            c = readChar();
        } else if (c == '+') {
            c = readChar();
        }

        int val = 0;
        while (c >= '0' && c <= '9') {
            val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
            c = readChar();
        }
        out = sign * val;
        return true;
    }

    bool readDouble(double& out) {
        char c = nextNonSpace();
        if (!c) return false;

        int sign = 1;
        if (c == '-') {
            sign = -1;
            c = readChar();
        } else if (c == '+') {
            c = readChar();
        }

        double val = 0.0;
        while (c >= '0' && c <= '9') {
            val = val * 10.0 + static_cast<double>(c - '0');
            c = readChar();
        }

        if (c == '.') {
            double place = 0.1;
            c = readChar();
            while (c >= '0' && c <= '9') {
                val += static_cast<double>(c - '0') * place;
                place *= 0.1;
                c = readChar();
            }
        }

        if (c == 'e' || c == 'E') {
            int expSign = 1;
            int expVal = 0;
            c = readChar();
            if (c == '-') {
                expSign = -1;
                c = readChar();
            } else if (c == '+') {
                c = readChar();
            }
            while (c >= '0' && c <= '9') {
                expVal = expVal * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = readChar();
            }
            val *= std::pow(10.0, static_cast<double>(expSign * expVal));
        }

        out = sign * val;
        return true;
    }

private:
    static constexpr size_t kBufSize = 1u << 20;
    char buffer_[kBufSize];
    size_t pos_;
    size_t len_;

    char readChar() {
        if (pos_ >= len_) {
            len_ = std::fread(buffer_, 1, kBufSize, stdin);
            pos_ = 0;
            if (len_ == 0) return 0;
        }
        return buffer_[pos_++];
    }

    char nextNonSpace() {
        char c = readChar();
        while (c && c <= ' ') c = readChar();
        return c;
    }
};

static inline double dist2(const std::vector<float>& xs, const std::vector<float>& ys, int a, int b) {
    const double dx = static_cast<double>(xs[a]) - static_cast<double>(xs[b]);
    const double dy = static_cast<double>(ys[a]) - static_cast<double>(ys[b]);
    return dx * dx + dy * dy;
}

std::vector<int> solveExactBruteforce(const std::vector<float>& xs, const std::vector<float>& ys) {
    const int n = static_cast<int>(xs.size());
    if (n <= 1) return {0};

    std::vector<std::vector<double>> d(n, std::vector<double>(n, 0.0));
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        for (int j = i + 1; j < n; ++j) {
            const double dist = std::sqrt(dist2(xs, ys, i, j));
            d[i][j] = dist;
            d[j][i] = dist;
        }
    }

    std::vector<int> perm(n - 1);
    std::iota(perm.begin(), perm.end(), 1);
    std::vector<int> bestPerm = perm;
    double bestLen = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

    do {
        double len = d[0][perm[0]];
        for (int i = 0; i + 1 < n - 1; ++i) {
            len += d[perm[i]][perm[i + 1]];
            if (len >= bestLen) break;
        }
        if (len < bestLen) {
            len += d[perm.back()][0];
            if (len < bestLen) {
                bestLen = len;
                bestPerm = perm;
            }
        }
    } while (std::next_permutation(perm.begin(), perm.end()));

    std::vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);
    route.push_back(0);
    route.insert(route.end(), bestPerm.begin(), bestPerm.end());
    return route;
}

std::vector<int> solveNearestNeighbor(const std::vector<float>& xs, const std::vector<float>& ys) {
    const int n = static_cast<int>(xs.size());
    std::vector<int> route;
    route.reserve(n);

    std::vector<unsigned char> used(n, 0);
    route.push_back(0);
    used[0] = 1;

    for (int step = 1; step < n; ++step) {
        const int cur = route.back();
        int best = -1;
        double bestD = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

        for (int j = 1; j < n; ++j) {
            if (used[j]) continue;
            const double d = dist2(xs, ys, cur, j);
            if (d < bestD) {
                bestD = d;
                best = j;
            }
        }

        used[best] = 1;
        route.push_back(best);
    }

    return route;
}

void twoOptLocal(std::vector<int>& route, const std::vector<float>& xs, const std::vector<float>& ys,
                 int window, int maxPasses) {
    const int n = static_cast<int>(route.size());
    if (n < 4) return;

    for (int pass = 0; pass < maxPasses; ++pass) {
        bool improved = false;

        for (int i = 1; i + 1 < n; ++i) {
            int a = route[i - 1];
            int b = route[i];
            const int maxK = std::min(n - 1, i + window);

            for (int k = i + 1; k <= maxK; ++k) {
                const int c = route[k];
                const int d = (k + 1 < n) ? route[k + 1] : route[0];

                const double oldDist = dist2(xs, ys, a, b) + dist2(xs, ys, c, d);
                const double newDist = dist2(xs, ys, a, c) + dist2(xs, ys, b, d);

                if (newDist + 1e-12 < oldDist) {
                    std::reverse(route.begin() + i, route.begin() + (k + 1));
                    improved = true;
                    a = route[i - 1];
                    b = route[i];
                }
            }
        }

        if (!improved) break;
    }
}

static inline uint64_t splitBy1(uint32_t x) {
    uint64_t v = x;
    v = (v | (v << 16)) & 0x0000FFFF0000FFFFULL;
    v = (v | (v << 8)) & 0x00FF00FF00FF00FFULL;
    v = (v | (v << 4)) & 0x0F0F0F0F0F0F0F0FULL;
    v = (v | (v << 2)) & 0x3333333333333333ULL;
    v = (v | (v << 1)) & 0x5555555555555555ULL;
    return v;
}

static inline uint64_t morton2D(uint32_t x, uint32_t y) {
    return splitBy1(x) | (splitBy1(y) << 1);
}

struct KeyIndex {
    uint64_t key;
    uint32_t idx;
};

std::vector<int> solveMorton(const std::vector<float>& xs, const std::vector<float>& ys) {
    const int n = static_cast<int>(xs.size());
    if (n <= 1) return {0};

    float minX = xs[0], maxX = xs[0], minY = ys[0], maxY = ys[0];
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        minX = std::min(minX, xs[i]);
        maxX = std::max(maxX, xs[i]);
        minY = std::min(minY, ys[i]);
        maxY = std::max(maxY, ys[i]);
    }

    constexpr double kMaxU32 = 4294967295.0;
    const double scaleX = (maxX > minX) ? (kMaxU32 / static_cast<double>(maxX - minX)) : 0.0;
    const double scaleY = (maxY > minY) ? (kMaxU32 / static_cast<double>(maxY - minY)) : 0.0;

    std::vector<KeyIndex> keys;
    keys.reserve(static_cast<size_t>(n - 1));

    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        double tx = (static_cast<double>(xs[i]) - static_cast<double>(minX)) * scaleX;
        double ty = (static_cast<double>(ys[i]) - static_cast<double>(minY)) * scaleY;

        if (tx < 0.0) tx = 0.0;
        if (tx > kMaxU32) tx = kMaxU32;
        if (ty < 0.0) ty = 0.0;
        if (ty > kMaxU32) ty = kMaxU32;

        const uint32_t qx = static_cast<uint32_t>(tx);
        const uint32_t qy = static_cast<uint32_t>(ty);
        keys.push_back(KeyIndex{morton2D(qx, qy), static_cast<uint32_t>(i)});
    }

    std::sort(keys.begin(), keys.end(), [](const KeyIndex& a, const KeyIndex& b) {
        if (a.key != b.key) return a.key < b.key;
        return a.idx < b.idx;
    });

    std::vector<int> route;
    route.resize(n);
    route[0] = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        route[i] = static_cast<int>(keys[i - 1].idx);
    }

    return route;
}

static inline void appendUInt(std::string& out, uint32_t v) {
    char tmp[16];
    int len = 0;
    do {
        tmp[len++] = static_cast<char>('0' + (v % 10));
        v /= 10;
    } while (v > 0);

    for (int i = len - 1; i >= 0; --i) out.push_back(tmp[i]);
}

void writeRoute(const std::vector<int>& route) {
    std::string out;
    out.reserve(1u << 20);

    for (size_t i = 0; i < route.size(); ++i) {
        if (i) out.push_back(' ');
        appendUInt(out, static_cast<uint32_t>(route[i]));

        if (out.size() >= (1u << 20)) {
            std::fwrite(out.data(), 1, out.size(), stdout);
            out.clear();
        }
    }

    out.push_back('\n');
    std::fwrite(out.data(), 1, out.size(), stdout);
}

int main() {
    FastInput in;

    int n = 0;
    if (!in.readInt(n)) return 0;
    if (n <= 0) {
        std::puts("");
        return 0;
    }

    std::vector<float> xs(static_cast<size_t>(n));
    std::vector<float> ys(static_cast<size_t>(n));

    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        double x = 0.0, y = 0.0;
        if (!in.readDouble(x)) x = 0.0;
        if (!in.readDouble(y)) y = 0.0;
        xs[i] = static_cast<float>(x);
        ys[i] = static_cast<float>(y);
    }

    std::vector<int> route;

    if (n <= 11) {
        route = solveExactBruteforce(xs, ys);
    } else if (n <= 4000) {
        route = solveNearestNeighbor(xs, ys);
        const int window = (n <= 1000) ? 56 : 28;
        const int passes = (n <= 1500) ? 3 : 2;
        twoOptLocal(route, xs, ys, window, passes);
    } else {
        route = solveMorton(xs, ys);
        if (n <= 200000) {
            const int window = (n <= 50000) ? 24 : 12;
            twoOptLocal(route, xs, ys, window, 1);
        }
    }

    writeRoute(route);
    return 0;
}

Test 2: Chinese Postman Problem Solver (C#) Score: 11.50 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Chinese Postman Problem (Route Inspection Problem) in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-20 nodes, 8-45 edges (small graphs, exact algorithms feasible)
- **Medium**: 35-200 nodes, 80-600 edges (requires efficient algorithms)
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, 2000-5000 edges (requires optimized implementations)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, 30000-80000 edges (requires very fast algorithms)
- **Epic**: 50000-100000 nodes, 500000-1000000 edges (requires highly optimized algorithms)

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 100000 nodes. The same function must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v w (edge endpoints, 0-indexed, weight integer)

**Output format (stdout):**
A sequence of node indices (whitespace-separated) representing a route that starts at node 0,
traverses EVERY edge at least once, and returns to node 0.
You may optionally prefix with a single integer L (route length). If present, it must match the number of nodes listed.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Correctness**: Must traverse every edge at least once

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Imagine a mail carrier who must walk along every street in a neighbourhood and return home. Some streets may need to be walked twice if the network doesn't allow a perfect loop — the goal is to minimise that extra walking.

The AI must figure out which streets (edges) to re-traverse and produce a complete route. Subpasses increase the graph size, making the problem harder to solve efficiently.

 1.0000
[6 nodes, 8 edges] Route distance: 511, Baseline: 684, Ratio: 0.75x, Time: 0.1s - excellent (within 5% of baseline)

Chinese Postman Problem - 6 nodes, 8 edges

View Code (7567 chars)
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Collections.Generic;

public static class Program
{
    private struct Edge
    {
        public int U;
        public int V;
        public int W;

        public Edge(int u, int v, int w)
        {
            U = u;
            V = v;
            W = w;
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _len;
        private int _ptr;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
        }

        private int ReadByte()
        {
            if (_ptr >= _len)
            {
                _len = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _ptr = 0;
                if (_len <= 0) return -1;
            }

            return _buffer[_ptr++];
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
                if (c == -1) return int.MinValue;
            } while (c <= 32);

            int sign = 1;
            if (c == '-')
            {
                sign = -1;
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            int val = 0;
            while (c > 32)
            {
                val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
                c = ReadByte();
            }

            return val * sign;
        }
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var fs = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());

        int n = fs.NextInt();
        if (n == int.MinValue)
        {
            return;
        }

        int m = fs.NextInt();
        if (n <= 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        if (m < 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        var edges = new Edge[m];
        var degree = new int[n];

        int baseArcCap = Math.Max(1, 2 * m);
        var head = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(head, -1);
        var to = new int[baseArcCap];
        var next = new int[baseArcCap];
        var arcEdgeId = new int[baseArcCap];
        int arcPtr = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int u = fs.NextInt();
            int v = fs.NextInt();
            int w = fs.NextInt();

            if ((uint)u >= (uint)n || (uint)v >= (uint)n)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("0");
                return;
            }

            edges[i] = new Edge(u, v, w);
            degree[u]++;
            degree[v]++;

            to[arcPtr] = v;
            arcEdgeId[arcPtr] = i;
            next[arcPtr] = head[u];
            head[u] = arcPtr++;

            to[arcPtr] = u;
            arcEdgeId[arcPtr] = i;
            next[arcPtr] = head[v];
            head[v] = arcPtr++;
        }

        if (m == 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        // DFS from node 0 to verify all edges are reachable and build a spanning tree.
        var visited = new bool[n];
        var parent = new int[n];
        var parentEdge = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(parent, -1);
        Array.Fill(parentEdge, -1);

        var dfsStack = new int[n];
        var order = new int[n];
        int sp = 0;
        int ordCount = 0;

        visited[0] = true;
        dfsStack[sp++] = 0;

        while (sp > 0)
        {
            int v = dfsStack[--sp];
            order[ordCount++] = v;

            for (int e = head[v]; e != -1; e = next[e])
            {
                int u = to[e];
                if (visited[u]) continue;

                visited[u] = true;
                parent[u] = v;
                parentEdge[u] = arcEdgeId[e];
                dfsStack[sp++] = u;
            }
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            if (!visited[edges[i].U] || !visited[edges[i].V])
            {
                // No feasible closed walk from node 0 can cover all edges.
                Console.WriteLine("0");
                return;
            }
        }

        // Eulerize by duplicating selected spanning-tree edges based on parity.
        var extraCountOnEdge = new int[m];
        var odd = new int[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            odd[i] = degree[i] & 1;
        }

        for (int i = ordCount - 1; i >= 1; i--)
        {
            int v = order[i];
            if (odd[v] == 0) continue;

            int pe = parentEdge[v];
            if (pe < 0)
            {
                Console.WriteLine("0");
                return;
            }

            extraCountOnEdge[pe]++;
            odd[v] ^= 1;
            odd[parent[v]] ^= 1;
        }

        if (odd[0] != 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        long extraTotalLong = 0;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            extraTotalLong += extraCountOnEdge[i];
        }

        long totalEdgesLong = (long)m + extraTotalLong;
        if (totalEdgesLong > int.MaxValue)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        int totalEdges = (int)totalEdgesLong;

        var mu = new int[totalEdges];
        var mv = new int[totalEdges];
        int p = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            mu[p] = edges[i].U;
            mv[p] = edges[i].V;
            p++;
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < m; i++)
        {
            int add = extraCountOnEdge[i];
            for (int c = 0; c < add; c++)
            {
                mu[p] = edges[i].U;
                mv[p] = edges[i].V;
                p++;
            }
        }

        int arcCap = Math.Max(1, 2 * totalEdges);
        var eHead = new int[n];
        Array.Fill(eHead, -1);
        var eTo = new int[arcCap];
        var eNext = new int[arcCap];
        var eId = new int[arcCap];
        int ep = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < totalEdges; i++)
        {
            int a = mu[i];
            int b = mv[i];

            eTo[ep] = b;
            eId[ep] = i;
            eNext[ep] = eHead[a];
            eHead[a] = ep++;

            eTo[ep] = a;
            eId[ep] = i;
            eNext[ep] = eHead[b];
            eHead[b] = ep++;
        }

        // Hierholzer on Eulerian multigraph.
        var iter = new int[n];
        Array.Copy(eHead, iter, n);

        var used = new bool[totalEdges];
        var pathStack = new int[totalEdges + 1];
        var circuit = new int[totalEdges + 1];
        int pathSp = 0;
        int circuitLen = 0;

        pathStack[pathSp++] = 0;

        while (pathSp > 0)
        {
            int v = pathStack[pathSp - 1];
            int e = iter[v];

            while (e != -1 && used[eId[e]])
            {
                e = eNext[e];
            }

            iter[v] = e;

            if (e == -1)
            {
                circuit[circuitLen++] = v;
                pathSp--;
            }
            else
            {
                int id = eId[e];
                used[id] = true;
                iter[v] = eNext[e];
                pathStack[pathSp++] = eTo[e];
            }
        }

        if (circuitLen != totalEdges + 1)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        using var writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput());
        for (int i = circuitLen - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            if (i != circuitLen - 1) writer.Write(' ');
            writer.Write(circuit[i]);
        }
        writer.WriteLine();
    }
}

Test 3: Graph Layout - Edge Crossing Minimization (Rust) Score: 3.00 / 16
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Graph Layout problem (Edge Crossing Minimization) in Rust.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY graph size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 6-15 nodes, small trees and cycles (planar, 0 crossings achievable)
- **Medium**: 20-100 nodes, dense planar and non-planar graphs
- **Large**: 500-1000 nodes, random dense graphs (many crossings unavoidable)
- **Extreme**: 5000-10000 nodes, very large random graphs

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with graphs ranging from 6 nodes to 200,000 nodes. The same solver must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N M (number of nodes, number of edges)
Lines 2..M+1: u v (undirected edge endpoints, 0-indexed)

**Output format (stdout):**
Output N lines, one per node, with: x y coordinates (floats).
You may optionally prefix with a single integer N on the first line.

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on graph size and density
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for large graphs
3. **Quality**: Should produce reasonable layouts with minimal crossings
4. **Robustness**: Handle disconnected components, self-loops, and parallel edges gracefully

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a graph (a set of dots connected by lines), arrange the dots on a 2D canvas so that as few lines cross each other as possible. A clean, readable layout is the goal — tangled spaghetti is the enemy.

Minimising crossings is extremely hard in general. The AI must place nodes intelligently; the baseline simply puts them in a circle. Subpasses increase the number of nodes and edges.

 1.0000
[tree, 6 nodes, 5 edges] Crossings: 0, Baseline: 1, Time: 0.0s - perfect (0 crossings)

Graph Layout - tree (6 nodes, 5 edges)

View Code (17292 chars)
use std::cmp::Ordering;
use std::f64::consts::PI;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

const EPS: f64 = 1e-9;
const UNSET: usize = usize::MAX;

struct FastInput {
    buf: Vec<u8>,
    idx: usize,
}

impl FastInput {
    fn new() -> Self {
        let mut buf = Vec::new();
        io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut buf).unwrap();
        Self { buf, idx: 0 }
    }

    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> Option<usize> {
        while self.idx < self.buf.len() && self.buf[self.idx].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.idx += 1;
        }
        if self.idx >= self.buf.len() {
            return None;
        }
        let mut val: usize = 0;
        let mut found = false;
        while self.idx < self.buf.len() {
            let b = self.buf[self.idx];
            if b.is_ascii_digit() {
                found = true;
                val = val.saturating_mul(10).saturating_add((b - b'0') as usize);
                self.idx += 1;
            } else {
                break;
            }
        }
        if !found {
            return None;
        }
        Some(val)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct XorShift64 {
    state: u64,
}

impl XorShift64 {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        let s = if seed == 0 {
            0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15
        } else {
            seed
        };
        Self { state: s }
    }

    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {
        let mut x = self.state;
        x ^= x << 7;
        x ^= x >> 9;
        x ^= x << 8;
        self.state = x;
        x
    }

    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        ((self.next_u64() >> 11) as f64) * (1.0 / ((1u64 << 53) as f64))
    }

    fn next_usize(&mut self, upper: usize) -> usize {
        if upper <= 1 {
            0
        } else {
            (self.next_u64() as usize) % upper
        }
    }
}

fn splitmix64(mut x: u64) -> u64 {
    x = x.wrapping_add(0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15);
    x = (x ^ (x >> 30)).wrapping_mul(0xBF58_476D_1CE4_E5B9);
    x = (x ^ (x >> 27)).wrapping_mul(0x94D0_49BB_1331_11EB);
    x ^ (x >> 31)
}

fn normalize_center(v: &mut [f64]) -> f64 {
    if v.is_empty() {
        return 0.0;
    }
    let mean = v.iter().sum::<f64>() / (v.len() as f64);
    let mut norm2 = 0.0;
    for x in v.iter_mut() {
        *x -= mean;
        norm2 += *x * *x;
    }
    let norm = norm2.sqrt();
    if norm > EPS {
        let inv = 1.0 / norm;
        for x in v.iter_mut() {
            *x *= inv;
        }
    }
    norm
}

fn orthogonalize(v: &mut [f64], base: &[f64]) {
    let mut dot = 0.0;
    for i in 0..v.len() {
        dot += v[i] * base[i];
    }
    for i in 0..v.len() {
        v[i] -= dot * base[i];
    }
}

fn build_local_edges(
    nodes: &[usize],
    offsets: &[usize],
    nbrs: &[usize],
    inv: &[usize],
) -> Vec<(u32, u32)> {
    let mut edges = Vec::with_capacity(nodes.len().saturating_mul(2));
    for (li, &v) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
        for &u in &nbrs[offsets[v]..offsets[v + 1]] {
            let lj = inv[u];
            if lj != UNSET && li < lj {
                edges.push((li as u32, lj as u32));
            }
        }
    }
    edges
}

fn init_positions(n: usize, seed: u64) -> (Vec<f64>, Vec<f64>) {
    let mut x = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0; n];
    if n == 0 {
        return (x, y);
    }
    let mut rng = XorShift64::new(seed);
    let r = (n as f64).sqrt().max(1.0);
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = 2.0 * PI * (i as f64) / (n as f64);
        x[i] = r * a.cos() + (rng.next_f64() - 0.5) * 0.2;
        y[i] = r * a.sin() + (rng.next_f64() - 0.5) * 0.2;
    }
    (x, y)
}

fn full_force_layout(n: usize, edges: &[(u32, u32)], seed: u64) -> (Vec<f64>, Vec<f64>) {
    if n <= 1 {
        return (vec![0.0; n], vec![0.0; n]);
    }
    let (mut x, mut y) = init_positions(n, seed);
    let mut fx = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut fy = vec![0.0; n];
    let iters = 220usize;
    let ideal = 2.2;
    let spring = 0.14;
    let repulse = 1.25;
    let gravity = 0.02 / (n as f64).sqrt().max(1.0);
    let mut step = 2.5 + (n as f64).sqrt() * 0.45;

    for it in 0..iters {
        fx.fill(0.0);
        fy.fill(0.0);

        for i in 0..n {
            for j in (i + 1)..n {
                let dx = x[i] - x[j];
                let dy = y[i] - y[j];
                let d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + EPS;
                let f = repulse / d2;
                let rx = dx * f;
                let ry = dy * f;
                fx[i] += rx;
                fy[i] += ry;
                fx[j] -= rx;
                fy[j] -= ry;
            }
        }

        for &(a, b) in edges {
            let i = a as usize;
            let j = b as usize;
            let dx = x[j] - x[i];
            let dy = y[j] - y[i];
            let d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + EPS;
            let d = d2.sqrt();
            let f = spring * (d - ideal);
            let ex = dx / d * f;
            let ey = dy / d * f;
            fx[i] += ex;
            fy[i] += ey;
            fx[j] -= ex;
            fy[j] -= ey;
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            fx[i] -= x[i] * gravity;
            fy[i] -= y[i] * gravity;
        }

        let cool = 1.0 - (it as f64) / (iters as f64);
        let temp = step * cool;
        for i in 0..n {
            let d2 = fx[i] * fx[i] + fy[i] * fy[i];
            if d2 > EPS {
                let d = d2.sqrt();
                let mv = temp.min(d);
                x[i] += fx[i] * (mv / d);
                y[i] += fy[i] * (mv / d);
            }
        }
        step *= 0.985;
    }

    (x, y)
}

fn sampled_force_layout(
    n: usize,
    edges: &[(u32, u32)],
    seed: u64,
    density: f64,
) -> (Vec<f64>, Vec<f64>) {
    if n <= 1 {
        return (vec![0.0; n], vec![0.0; n]);
    }
    let (mut x, mut y) = init_positions(n, seed ^ 0xA5A5_A5A5_A5A5_A5A5);
    let mut fx = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut fy = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut rng = XorShift64::new(seed ^ 0xD1B5_4A32_D192_ED03);

    let iters = if n <= 300 {
        180
    } else if n <= 1200 {
        120
    } else {
        84
    };
    let samples = if n <= 700 {
        28
    } else if n <= 2000 {
        20
    } else {
        14
    };

    let edge_count = edges.len().max(1) as f64;
    let ideal = ((n as f64 / edge_count).sqrt() * 2.7).clamp(0.9, 6.0);
    let spring = 0.085;
    let repulse = 1.1;
    let gravity = (0.006 + density * 0.01).clamp(0.006, 0.03);
    let scale = (n as f64) / (samples as f64);
    let mut step = ideal * 2.4 + 1.0;

    for it in 0..iters {
        fx.fill(0.0);
        fy.fill(0.0);

        for i in 0..n {
            for _ in 0..samples {
                let mut j = rng.next_usize(n - 1);
                if j >= i {
                    j += 1;
                }
                let dx = x[i] - x[j];
                let dy = y[i] - y[j];
                let d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + EPS;
                let f = repulse * scale / d2;
                fx[i] += dx * f;
                fy[i] += dy * f;
            }
        }

        for &(a, b) in edges {
            let i = a as usize;
            let j = b as usize;
            let dx = x[j] - x[i];
            let dy = y[j] - y[i];
            let d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy + EPS;
            let d = d2.sqrt();
            let f = spring * (d - ideal);
            let ex = dx / d * f;
            let ey = dy / d * f;
            fx[i] += ex;
            fy[i] += ey;
            fx[j] -= ex;
            fy[j] -= ey;
        }

        for i in 0..n {
            fx[i] -= x[i] * gravity;
            fy[i] -= y[i] * gravity;
        }

        let cool = 1.0 - (it as f64) / (iters as f64);
        let temp = step * (0.25 + 0.75 * cool);
        for i in 0..n {
            let d2 = fx[i] * fx[i] + fy[i] * fy[i];
            if d2 > EPS {
                let d = d2.sqrt();
                let mv = temp.min(d);
                x[i] += fx[i] * (mv / d);
                y[i] += fy[i] * (mv / d);
            }
        }
        step *= 0.987;
    }

    (x, y)
}

fn matvec_norm_adj(
    nodes: &[usize],
    offsets: &[usize],
    nbrs: &[usize],
    inv: &[usize],
    inv_sqrt_deg: &[f64],
    src: &[f64],
    dst: &mut [f64],
) {
    for (li, &v) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
        let mut sum = 0.0;
        let sv = inv_sqrt_deg[v];
        for &u in &nbrs[offsets[v]..offsets[v + 1]] {
            let lj = inv[u];
            if lj != UNSET {
                sum += src[lj] * inv_sqrt_deg[u];
            }
        }
        dst[li] = sum * sv;
    }
}

fn spectral_layout(
    nodes: &[usize],
    offsets: &[usize],
    nbrs: &[usize],
    inv: &[usize],
    inv_sqrt_deg: &[f64],
    seed: u64,
    m_edges: usize,
) -> (Vec<f64>, Vec<f64>) {
    let n = nodes.len();
    if n <= 1 {
        return (vec![0.0; n], vec![0.0; n]);
    }

    let mut v1 = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut v2 = vec![0.0; n];
    let mut w = vec![0.0; n];

    const INV53: f64 = 1.0 / ((1u64 << 53) as f64);

    for i in 0..n {
        let h = splitmix64(seed ^ (nodes[i] as u64).wrapping_mul(0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15));
        v1[i] = ((h >> 11) as f64) * INV53 - 0.5;
    }
    if normalize_center(&mut v1) <= EPS {
        for i in 0..n {
            v1[i] = (i as f64) / (n as f64) - 0.5;
        }
        normalize_center(&mut v1);
    }

    let m = m_edges.max(1);
    let mut iters = (120_000_000usize / m.saturating_mul(2)).clamp(4, 24);
    if n > 80_000 {
        iters = iters.min(10);
    }
    if n > 150_000 {
        iters = iters.min(8);
    }
    if m > n.saturating_mul(40) {
        iters = iters.min(6);
    }

    for _ in 0..iters {
        matvec_norm_adj(nodes, offsets, nbrs, inv, inv_sqrt_deg, &v1, &mut w);
        if normalize_center(&mut w) <= EPS {
            break;
        }
        std::mem::swap(&mut v1, &mut w);
    }

    for i in 0..n {
        let h =
            splitmix64(seed ^ 0xD1B5_4A32_D192_ED03 ^ (nodes[i] as u64).wrapping_mul(0x94D0_49BB_1331_11EB));
        v2[i] = ((h >> 11) as f64) * INV53 - 0.5;
    }
    orthogonalize(&mut v2, &v1);
    if normalize_center(&mut v2) <= EPS {
        for i in 0..n {
            v2[i] = ((i * 13 % (n + 1)) as f64) / (n as f64) - 0.5;
        }
        orthogonalize(&mut v2, &v1);
        normalize_center(&mut v2);
    }

    for _ in 0..iters {
        matvec_norm_adj(nodes, offsets, nbrs, inv, inv_sqrt_deg, &v2, &mut w);
        orthogonalize(&mut w, &v1);
        if normalize_center(&mut w) <= EPS {
            break;
        }
        std::mem::swap(&mut v2, &mut w);
    }

    if normalize_center(&mut v2) <= EPS {
        for i in 0..n {
            let h = splitmix64(seed ^ 0xA5A5_A5A5_A5A5_A5A5 ^ (nodes[i] as u64));
            v2[i] = ((h >> 11) as f64) * INV53 - 0.5;
        }
        orthogonalize(&mut v2, &v1);
        normalize_center(&mut v2);
    }

    let spread = (n as f64).sqrt().max(1.0) * 2.0;
    for i in 0..n {
        let h1 = splitmix64(seed ^ (nodes[i] as u64).wrapping_mul(0xBF58_476D_1CE4_E5B9));
        let h2 = splitmix64(seed ^ (nodes[i] as u64).wrapping_mul(0x94D0_49BB_1331_11EB));
        let jx = (((h1 >> 11) as f64) * INV53 - 0.5) * 0.02;
        let jy = (((h2 >> 11) as f64) * INV53 - 0.5) * 0.02;
        v1[i] = v1[i] * spread + jx;
        v2[i] = v2[i] * spread + jy;
    }

    (v1, v2)
}

fn component_bbox(nodes: &[usize], x: &[f64], y: &[f64]) -> (f64, f64, f64, f64) {
    if nodes.is_empty() {
        return (0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0);
    }
    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    for &v in nodes {
        min_x = min_x.min(x[v]);
        max_x = max_x.max(x[v]);
        min_y = min_y.min(y[v]);
        max_y = max_y.max(y[v]);
    }
    (min_x, max_x, min_y, max_y)
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = FastInput::new();
    let n = match input.next_usize() {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => return,
    };
    let m = input.next_usize().unwrap_or(0);

    if n == 0 {
        return;
    }

    let mut edges = Vec::<(usize, usize)>::with_capacity(m);
    let mut deg = vec![0usize; n];

    for _ in 0..m {
        let u = match input.next_usize() {
            Some(v) => v,
            None => break,
        };
        let v = match input.next_usize() {
            Some(v) => v,
            None => break,
        };
        if u >= n || v >= n {
            continue;
        }
        if u == v {
            continue;
        }
        deg[u] += 1;
        deg[v] += 1;
        edges.push((u, v));
    }

    let mut offsets = vec![0usize; n + 1];
    for i in 0..n {
        offsets[i + 1] = offsets[i] + deg[i];
    }

    let mut nbrs = vec![0usize; offsets[n]];
    let mut cur = offsets[..n].to_vec();
    for &(u, v) in &edges {
        nbrs[cur[u]] = v;
        cur[u] += 1;
        nbrs[cur[v]] = u;
        cur[v] += 1;
    }

    let mut inv_sqrt_deg = vec![0.0; n];
    for i in 0..n {
        inv_sqrt_deg[i] = if deg[i] == 0 {
            0.0
        } else {
            1.0 / (deg[i] as f64).sqrt()
        };
    }

    let mut comp_id = vec![UNSET; n];
    let mut components: Vec<Vec<usize>> = Vec::new();
    let mut comp_edges: Vec<usize> = Vec::new();
    let mut queue = Vec::<usize>::new();

    for start in 0..n {
        if comp_id[start] != UNSET {
            continue;
        }
        let cid = components.len();
        queue.clear();
        queue.push(start);
        comp_id[start] = cid;
        let mut head = 0usize;
        let mut nodes = Vec::new();
        let mut deg_sum = 0usize;

        while head < queue.len() {
            let v = queue[head];
            head += 1;
            nodes.push(v);
            let s = offsets[v];
            let e = offsets[v + 1];
            deg_sum += e - s;
            for &u in &nbrs[s..e] {
                if comp_id[u] == UNSET {
                    comp_id[u] = cid;
                    queue.push(u);
                }
            }
        }

        components.push(nodes);
        comp_edges.push(deg_sum / 2);
    }

    let mut x = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut y = vec![0.0f64; n];
    let mut inv = vec![UNSET; n];
    let mut comp_boxes = vec![(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0); components.len()];

    for cid in 0..components.len() {
        let nodes = &components[cid];
        let nc = nodes.len();
        for (li, &v) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
            inv[v] = li;
        }

        if nc == 1 {
            let v = nodes[0];
            x[v] = 0.0;
            y[v] = 0.0;
            comp_boxes[cid] = (-0.5, 0.5, -0.5, 0.5);
            inv[v] = UNSET;
            continue;
        }

        let mc = comp_edges[cid];
        let density = (2.0 * mc as f64) / ((nc * (nc - 1)) as f64);
        let seed = splitmix64((cid as u64).wrapping_mul(0x9E37_79B9_7F4A_7C15) ^ (nc as u64));

        let (lx, ly) = if nc <= 80 {
            let local_edges = build_local_edges(nodes, &offsets, &nbrs, &inv);
            full_force_layout(nc, &local_edges, seed)
        } else if nc <= 4000 && mc <= nc.saturating_mul(50) {
            let local_edges = build_local_edges(nodes, &offsets, &nbrs, &inv);
            sampled_force_layout(nc, &local_edges, seed, density)
        } else {
            spectral_layout(nodes, &offsets, &nbrs, &inv, &inv_sqrt_deg, seed, mc)
        };

        for (i, &v) in nodes.iter().enumerate() {
            x[v] = lx[i];
            y[v] = ly[i];
        }

        comp_boxes[cid] = component_bbox(nodes, &x, &y);

        for &v in nodes {
            inv[v] = UNSET;
        }
    }

    let mut order: Vec<usize> = (0..components.len()).collect();
    let area = |b: (f64, f64, f64, f64)| {
        let w = (b.1 - b.0).max(1.0);
        let h = (b.3 - b.2).max(1.0);
        w * h
    };
    order.sort_by(|&a, &b| {
        area(comp_boxes[b])
            .partial_cmp(&area(comp_boxes[a]))
            .unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)
    });

    let pad = 4.0;
    let total_area: f64 = comp_boxes
        .iter()
        .map(|&b| {
            let w = (b.1 - b.0).max(1.0) + pad;
            let h = (b.3 - b.2).max(1.0) + pad;
            w * h
        })
        .sum();
    let target_row_width = total_area.sqrt().max(10.0);

    let mut cx = 0.0;
    let mut cy = 0.0;
    let mut row_h: f64 = 0.0;

    for &cid in &order {
        let b = comp_boxes[cid];
        let w = (b.1 - b.0).max(1.0);
        let h = (b.3 - b.2).max(1.0);

        if cx > 0.0 && cx + w > target_row_width {
            cx = 0.0;
            cy += row_h + pad;
            row_h = 0.0;
        }

        let ox = cx - b.0;
        let oy = cy - b.2;
        for &v in &components[cid] {
            x[v] += ox;
            y[v] += oy;
        }

        cx += w + pad;
        row_h = row_h.max(h);
    }

    let mut min_x = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_x = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut min_y = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut max_y = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    for i in 0..n {
        min_x = min_x.min(x[i]);
        max_x = max_x.max(x[i]);
        min_y = min_y.min(y[i]);
        max_y = max_y.max(y[i]);
    }
    let mid_x = 0.5 * (min_x + max_x);
    let mid_y = 0.5 * (min_y + max_y);
    for i in 0..n {
        x[i] -= mid_x;
        y[i] -= mid_y;
    }

    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    for i in 0..n {
        let _ = writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", x[i], y[i]);
    }
}

Test 4: Regular Tetrahedron Packing in Polyhedra (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Packing problem: Pack regular tetrahedrons into polyhedra (C++).

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY container size from trivial to ludicrous scale.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V edge_length
Lines 2..V+1: x y z (container vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: K (number of tetrahedrons placed)
Lines 2..K+1: cx cy cz rx ry rz (center + Euler angles in radians)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container volume and complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize number of tetrahedrons while ensuring no overlaps

**Key Geometry (edge length e=1.0):**
- Height: h = e * sqrt(2/3) ≈ 0.8165
- Circumradius: R = e * sqrt(3/8) ≈ 0.6124
- Inradius: r = e / sqrt(24) ≈ 0.2041

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack as many identical triangular pyramids (tetrahedra) as possible inside a given 3D shape without any overlaps. Unlike cubes, tetrahedra cannot tile space perfectly, so clever placement and rotation are essential.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and geometry. Subpasses use increasingly complex container shapes. The baseline uses a simple grid placement strategy.

 0.0000
[small_cube] Packed: 0 legal tetrahedrons (of 0 total placements), Baseline: 34, Expected min: 1, Time: 0.1s - no valid tetrahedrons

Tetrahedron Packing - small_cube (8 vertices)

View Code (13450 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0.0;
    double y = 0.0;
    double z = 0.0;
};

struct Plane {
    Vec3 n;
    double d = 0.0;
};

struct BBox {
    Vec3 mn;
    Vec3 mx;
};

static inline Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z};
}

static inline Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y, a.z - b.z};
}

static inline Vec3 operator*(const Vec3& a, double s) {
    return {a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s};
}

static inline Vec3 operator/(const Vec3& a, double s) {
    return {a.x / s, a.y / s, a.z / s};
}

static inline Vec3& operator+=(Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    a.x += b.x;
    a.y += b.y;
    a.z += b.z;
    return a;
}

static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z;
}

static inline Vec3 cross(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return {
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x
    };
}

static inline double norm2(const Vec3& v) {
    return dot(v, v);
}

static inline double norm(const Vec3& v) {
    return std::sqrt(norm2(v));
}

static BBox computeBBox(const std::vector<Vec3>& pts) {
    const double inf = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
    BBox box{{inf, inf, inf}, {-inf, -inf, -inf}};
    for (const auto& p : pts) {
        box.mn.x = std::min(box.mn.x, p.x);
        box.mn.y = std::min(box.mn.y, p.y);
        box.mn.z = std::min(box.mn.z, p.z);
        box.mx.x = std::max(box.mx.x, p.x);
        box.mx.y = std::max(box.mx.y, p.y);
        box.mx.z = std::max(box.mx.z, p.z);
    }
    return box;
}

static double diagonalLength(const BBox& box) {
    return norm(box.mx - box.mn);
}

static std::vector<Vec3> pickRepresentativePoints(const std::vector<Vec3>& pts) {
    const std::size_t n = pts.size();
    if (n <= 140) {
        return pts;
    }

    std::vector<char> used(n, 0);
    std::vector<std::size_t> ids;
    ids.reserve(220);

    auto add = [&](std::size_t idx) {
        if (!used[idx]) {
            used[idx] = 1;
            ids.push_back(idx);
        }
    };

    std::size_t minX = 0, maxX = 0;
    std::size_t minY = 0, maxY = 0;
    std::size_t minZ = 0, maxZ = 0;

    for (std::size_t i = 1; i < n; ++i) {
        if (pts[i].x < pts[minX].x) minX = i;
        if (pts[i].x > pts[maxX].x) maxX = i;
        if (pts[i].y < pts[minY].y) minY = i;
        if (pts[i].y > pts[maxY].y) maxY = i;
        if (pts[i].z < pts[minZ].z) minZ = i;
        if (pts[i].z > pts[maxZ].z) maxZ = i;
    }

    add(minX); add(maxX);
    add(minY); add(maxY);
    add(minZ); add(maxZ);

    Vec3 centroid{};
    for (const auto& p : pts) {
        centroid += p;
    }
    centroid = centroid / static_cast<double>(n);

    int dirCount = 36;
    if (n < 5000) dirCount = 84;
    else if (n < 50000) dirCount = 56;

    const double goldenAngle = 2.39996322972865332223;
    for (int k = 0; k < dirCount; ++k) {
        const double z = 1.0 - 2.0 * ((static_cast<double>(k) + 0.5) / static_cast<double>(dirCount));
        const double r = std::sqrt(std::max(0.0, 1.0 - z * z));
        const double phi = goldenAngle * static_cast<double>(k);
        const Vec3 d{r * std::cos(phi), r * std::sin(phi), z};

        std::size_t maxIdx = 0;
        std::size_t minIdx = 0;
        double maxVal = -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
        double minVal =  std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

        for (std::size_t i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
            const double v = dot(pts[i] - centroid, d);
            if (v > maxVal) {
                maxVal = v;
                maxIdx = i;
            }
            if (v < minVal) {
                minVal = v;
                minIdx = i;
            }
        }

        add(maxIdx);
        add(minIdx);
    }

    constexpr std::size_t kMaxRep = 170;
    if (ids.size() > kMaxRep) {
        std::vector<std::pair<double, std::size_t>> scored;
        scored.reserve(ids.size());
        for (std::size_t idx : ids) {
            scored.push_back({norm2(pts[idx] - centroid), idx});
        }
        std::sort(scored.begin(), scored.end(), [](const auto& a, const auto& b) {
            return a.first > b.first;
        });

        std::vector<char> keep(n, 0);
        std::vector<std::size_t> trimmed;
        trimmed.reserve(kMaxRep);

        auto addKeep = [&](std::size_t idx) {
            if (!keep[idx] && trimmed.size() < kMaxRep) {
                keep[idx] = 1;
                trimmed.push_back(idx);
            }
        };

        addKeep(minX); addKeep(maxX);
        addKeep(minY); addKeep(maxY);
        addKeep(minZ); addKeep(maxZ);

        for (const auto& item : scored) {
            if (trimmed.size() >= kMaxRep) break;
            addKeep(item.second);
        }

        ids.swap(trimmed);
    }

    std::vector<Vec3> out;
    out.reserve(ids.size());
    for (std::size_t idx : ids) {
        out.push_back(pts[idx]);
    }
    return out;
}

static bool almostSamePlane(const Plane& a, const Plane& b, double normalTol, double dTol) {
    const double align = dot(a.n, b.n);
    return align > (1.0 - normalTol) && std::fabs(a.d - b.d) <= dTol;
}

static std::vector<Plane> buildConvexPlanes(const std::vector<Vec3>& pts) {
    std::vector<Plane> planes;
    const std::size_t n = pts.size();
    if (n < 4) {
        return planes;
    }

    const BBox box = computeBBox(pts);
    const double scale = std::max(1.0, diagonalLength(box));
    const double eps = 1e-9 * scale;
    const double dTol = 1e-7 * scale;
    const double normalTol = 1e-6;

    planes.reserve(256);

    for (std::size_t i = 0; i + 2 < n; ++i) {
        for (std::size_t j = i + 1; j + 1 < n; ++j) {
            for (std::size_t k = j + 1; k < n; ++k) {
                Vec3 normal = cross(pts[j] - pts[i], pts[k] - pts[i]);
                const double len = norm(normal);
                if (len <= eps) {
                    continue;
                }
                normal = normal / len;
                double d = dot(normal, pts[i]);

                bool seenPos = false;
                bool seenNeg = false;
                double maxSide = -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

                for (std::size_t t = 0; t < n; ++t) {
                    const double s = dot(normal, pts[t]) - d;
                    maxSide = std::max(maxSide, s);
                    if (s > eps) seenPos = true;
                    if (s < -eps) seenNeg = true;
                    if (seenPos && seenNeg) break;
                }

                if (seenPos && seenNeg) {
                    continue;
                }

                if (maxSide > eps) {
                    normal = normal * -1.0;
                    d = -d;
                }

                const Plane candidate{normal, d};
                bool duplicate = false;
                for (const auto& p : planes) {
                    if (almostSamePlane(candidate, p, normalTol, dTol)) {
                        duplicate = true;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (!duplicate) {
                    planes.push_back(candidate);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return planes;
}

static bool insideShrunkenPoly(const Vec3& c, const std::vector<Plane>& planes, double shrink, double eps) {
    for (const auto& pl : planes) {
        if (dot(pl.n, c) > (pl.d - shrink + eps)) {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    int V = 0;
    double edge = 0.0;
    if (!(std::cin >> V >> edge)) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    if (V <= 0 || edge <= 0.0) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    std::vector<Vec3> vertices;
    vertices.resize(static_cast<std::size_t>(V));
    for (int i = 0; i < V; ++i) {
        std::cin >> vertices[static_cast<std::size_t>(i)].x
                 >> vertices[static_cast<std::size_t>(i)].y
                 >> vertices[static_cast<std::size_t>(i)].z;
    }

    if (vertices.size() < 4) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const auto tStart = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
    const auto tDeadline = tStart + std::chrono::milliseconds(28500);

    const std::vector<Vec3> reduced = pickRepresentativePoints(vertices);
    std::vector<Plane> planes = buildConvexPlanes(reduced);

    if (planes.size() < 4 && reduced.size() < vertices.size() && vertices.size() <= 500) {
        planes = buildConvexPlanes(vertices);
    }

    if (planes.size() < 4) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const BBox box = computeBBox(reduced);
    const double boxScale = std::max(1.0, diagonalLength(box));

    // Circumradius of regular tetrahedron with edge length e.
    const double R = edge * std::sqrt(3.0 / 8.0);
    const double safety = 1.00001;
    const double inflatedR = R * safety;

    // FCC lattice with nearest-neighbor distance 2R.
    const double fccBaseStep = 2.0 * std::sqrt(2.0) * inflatedR;
    if (!(fccBaseStep > 0.0)) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const double dx = std::max(0.0, box.mx.x - box.mn.x);
    const double dy = std::max(0.0, box.mx.y - box.mn.y);
    const double dz = std::max(0.0, box.mx.z - box.mn.z);

    const double approxRawCandidates = 4.0 *
        (dx / fccBaseStep + 1.0) *
        (dy / fccBaseStep + 1.0) *
        (dz / fccBaseStep + 1.0);

    constexpr double kTargetCandidates = 2400000.0;
    double scaleFactor = 1.0;
    if (approxRawCandidates > kTargetCandidates) {
        scaleFactor = std::cbrt(approxRawCandidates / kTargetCandidates);
    }

    const double step = fccBaseStep * scaleFactor;
    if (!(step > 0.0)) {
        std::cout << 0 << '\n';
        return 0;
    }

    const double epsInside = 1e-9 * boxScale;

    constexpr std::size_t kHardCap = 250000;
    std::vector<Vec3> bestCenters;
    bestCenters.reserve(65536);

    std::array<Vec3, 4> shiftFractions{{
        {0.00, 0.00, 0.00},
        {0.25, 0.25, 0.25},
        {0.50, 0.00, 0.50},
        {0.00, 0.50, 0.50}
    }};

    int shiftsToTry = (approxRawCandidates <= 700000.0) ? 4 : 1;

    const std::array<Vec3, 4> basis{{
        {0.0, 0.0, 0.0},
        {0.0, 0.5, 0.5},
        {0.5, 0.0, 0.5},
        {0.5, 0.5, 0.0}
    }};

    for (int s = 0; s < shiftsToTry; ++s) {
        if (std::chrono::steady_clock::now() >= tDeadline) break;

        const Vec3 shift = {
            shiftFractions[static_cast<std::size_t>(s)].x * step,
            shiftFractions[static_cast<std::size_t>(s)].y * step,
            shiftFractions[static_cast<std::size_t>(s)].z * step
        };

        const Vec3 origin = {box.mn.x + shift.x, box.mn.y + shift.y, box.mn.z + shift.z};

        const int ix0 = static_cast<int>(std::floor((box.mn.x - origin.x) / step)) - 1;
        const int iy0 = static_cast<int>(std::floor((box.mn.y - origin.y) / step)) - 1;
        const int iz0 = static_cast<int>(std::floor((box.mn.z - origin.z) / step)) - 1;

        const int ix1 = static_cast<int>(std::ceil((box.mx.x - origin.x) / step)) + 1;
        const int iy1 = static_cast<int>(std::ceil((box.mx.y - origin.y) / step)) + 1;
        const int iz1 = static_cast<int>(std::ceil((box.mx.z - origin.z) / step)) + 1;

        std::vector<Vec3> current;
        current.reserve(bestCenters.size());

        std::uint64_t iterCounter = 0;
        bool timeUp = false;

        for (int ix = ix0; ix <= ix1 && !timeUp; ++ix) {
            const double bx = origin.x + step * static_cast<double>(ix);
            for (int iy = iy0; iy <= iy1 && !timeUp; ++iy) {
                const double by = origin.y + step * static_cast<double>(iy);
                for (int iz = iz0; iz <= iz1; ++iz) {
                    const double bz = origin.z + step * static_cast<double>(iz);

                    for (const auto& b : basis) {
                        const Vec3 c{bx + step * b.x, by + step * b.y, bz + step * b.z};
                        if (insideShrunkenPoly(c, planes, inflatedR, epsInside)) {
                            current.push_back(c);
                            if (current.size() >= kHardCap) {
                                timeUp = true;
                                break;
                            }
                        }
                    }

                    ++iterCounter;
                    if ((iterCounter & 0x3FFFu) == 0u) {
                        if (std::chrono::steady_clock::now() >= tDeadline) {
                            timeUp = true;
                            break;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        if (current.size() > bestCenters.size()) {
            bestCenters.swap(current);
        }
    }

    std::cout << bestCenters.size() << '\n';
    std::cout << std::fixed << std::setprecision(9);
    for (const auto& c : bestCenters) {
        // We use one fixed orientation; geometric validity comes from circumsphere-based constraints.
        std::cout << c.x << ' ' << c.y << ' ' << c.z << ' '
                  << 0.0 << ' ' << 0.0 << ' ' << 0.0 << '\n';
    }

    return 0;
}
