Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function. Assume only the standard library is available.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 1.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

 1.0000
[100x100x50 - simple 3D landing] End: landed, Distance: 2.7m, Time: 137.60s

3D Lunar Lander - 100x100x50 - simple 3D landing

View C++ Code (14399 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <fstream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <mutex>
#include <string>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

namespace {

constexpr double kPi = 3.14159265358979323846264338327950288;

template <typename T>
T clampValue(T v, T lo, T hi) {
    return std::max(lo, std::min(hi, v));
}

double wrapAngle(double a) {
    while (a > kPi) a -= 2.0 * kPi;
    while (a < -kPi) a += 2.0 * kPi;
    return a;
}

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0.0;
    double y = 0.0;
    double z = 0.0;

    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double x_, double y_, double z_) : x(x_), y(y_), z(z_) {}

    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3(x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z); }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3(x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z); }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return Vec3(x * s, y * s, z * s); }
    Vec3 operator/(double s) const { return Vec3(x / s, y / s, z / s); }
};

double norm2D(double x, double y) {
    return std::sqrt(x * x + y * y);
}

double norm3D(const Vec3& v) {
    return std::sqrt(v.x * v.x + v.y * v.y + v.z * v.z);
}

struct World {
    double width = 0.0;
    double depth = 0.0;
    double height = 0.0;
    double gravity = 1.62;
    double maxThrust = 0.0;
    double fuel0 = 0.0;

    Vec3 start;
    Vec3 target;

    double landingRadius = 5.0;
};

struct State {
    double x = 0.0;
    double y = 0.0;
    double z = 0.0;

    double vx = 0.0;
    double vy = 0.0;
    double vz = 0.0;

    double pitch = 0.0;
    double yaw = 0.0;
    double roll = 0.0;

    double pitchRate = 0.0;
    double yawRate = 0.0;
    double rollRate = 0.0;

    double fuel = 0.0;

    uint64_t seq = 0;
    std::chrono::steady_clock::time_point stamp;
};

struct Command {
    double thrust = 0.0;
    double pitchCmd = 0.0;
    double yawCmd = 0.0;
    double rollCmd = 0.0;
};

struct TerrainSummary {
    std::atomic<bool> ready{false};
    std::atomic<double> maxTop{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetA{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetB{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetC{0.0};
    std::atomic<bool> targetFound{false};
};

class Controller {
public:
    explicit Controller(const World& w)
        : world_(w) {
        prev_.thrust = clampValue(world_.gravity / std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust), 0.0, 1.0);
    }

    Command hoverCommand() const {
        Command c;
        c.thrust = clampValue(world_.gravity / std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust), 0.0, 1.0);
        c.pitchCmd = 0.0;
        c.yawCmd = 0.0;
        c.rollCmd = 0.0;
        return c;
    }

    Command compute(const State& s, const TerrainSummary& terrain) {
        const double g = world_.gravity;
        const double maxT = std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust);

        const double dx = world_.target.x - s.x;
        const double dy = world_.target.y - s.y;
        const double dxy = norm2D(dx, dy);
        const double alt = s.z - world_.target.z;

        const double maxTop = terrain.maxTop.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
        const bool terrainReady = terrain.ready.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

        const double safeMargin = std::max(8.0, 0.02 * world_.height);
        double safeZ = std::max({world_.start.z + 20.0, world_.target.z + 20.0, maxTop + safeMargin});
        safeZ = clampValue(safeZ, world_.target.z + 5.0, world_.height - 2.0);

        const double R = world_.landingRadius;

        double zRef;
        if (!terrainReady) {
            double provisional = std::max(world_.start.z, world_.target.z) + std::max(20.0, 0.1 * world_.height);
            zRef = clampValue(provisional, world_.target.z + 5.0, world_.height - 2.0);
        } else if (dxy > 6.0 * R) {
            zRef = safeZ;
        } else if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            zRef = clampValue(world_.target.z + std::max(6.0, 0.40 * dxy), world_.target.z + 2.0, safeZ);
        } else {
            zRef = world_.target.z + 0.6;
        }

        const double dirNorm = std::max(1e-9, dxy);
        const double dirX = dx / dirNorm;
        const double dirY = dy / dirNorm;

        double brakeA = std::max(0.9, 0.25 * (maxT - g));
        if (brakeA < 0.9) brakeA = 0.9;

        double speedCap;
        if (dxy > 6.0 * R) {
            speedCap = clampValue(0.06 * std::min(world_.width, world_.depth), 10.0, 120.0);
        } else if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            speedCap = 20.0;
        } else {
            speedCap = 4.0;
        }

        double vRefXY = std::min(speedCap, std::sqrt(std::max(0.0, 2.0 * brakeA * std::max(0.0, dxy - R))));
        if (dxy < 0.5 * R) vRefXY = std::min(vRefXY, 1.0);

        double vRefX = dirX * vRefXY;
        double vRefY = dirY * vRefXY;

        double vRefZ;
        if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            vRefZ = clampValue((zRef - s.z) * 0.60, -8.0, 8.0);
        } else {
            double desiredDown = clampValue(0.25 + 0.12 * std::max(0.0, alt), 0.2, 2.0);
            if (dxy > 0.8 * R) desiredDown = std::min(desiredDown, 0.8);
            if (alt < 1.2) desiredDown = std::min(desiredDown, 0.35);
            vRefZ = (alt > 0.0) ? -desiredDown : 0.4;
            if (norm2D(s.vx, s.vy) > 3.0) vRefZ = std::max(vRefZ, -0.3);
        }

        double kPxy = (dxy > 2.0 * R) ? 0.015 : 0.050;
        double kVxy = (dxy > 2.0 * R) ? 0.80 : 1.20;

        double ax = kVxy * (vRefX - s.vx) + kPxy * dx;
        double ay = kVxy * (vRefY - s.vy) + kPxy * dy;

        double kPz = 0.09;
        double kVz = 1.40;
        double az = kVz * (vRefZ - s.vz) + kPz * (zRef - s.z);

        double aXYLim = std::max(0.8, 0.55 * maxT);
        if (alt < 10.0) aXYLim *= 0.5;

        const double axy = norm2D(ax, ay);
        if (axy > aXYLim && axy > 1e-9) {
            const double sxy = aXYLim / axy;
            ax *= sxy;
            ay *= sxy;
        }

        const double azUpLim = std::max(0.4, maxT - g - 0.2);
        az = clampValue(az, -0.95 * g, azUpLim);

        if (alt < 2.0) {
            double neededUp = (-s.vz - 0.6) * 1.2;
            if (neededUp > az) az = neededUp;
            az = clampValue(az, -0.40 * g, azUpLim);
        }

        Vec3 thrustVec(ax, ay, az + g);
        if (thrustVec.z < 0.05) thrustVec.z = 0.05;

        double thrustMag = norm3D(thrustVec);
        const double maxUsableThrust = 0.98 * maxT;
        if (thrustMag > maxUsableThrust && thrustMag > 1e-9) {
            const double scale = maxUsableThrust / thrustMag;
            thrustVec = thrustVec * scale;
            thrustMag = maxUsableThrust;
        }

        double thrust = clampValue(thrustMag / maxT, 0.0, 1.0);

        const double h = norm2D(thrustVec.x, thrustVec.y);
        double pitchDes = std::atan2(h, std::max(1e-6, thrustVec.z));
        double yawDes = (h > 1e-9) ? std::atan2(-thrustVec.y, -thrustVec.x) : s.yaw;

        if (dxy < R && alt < 5.0) {
            pitchDes = std::min(pitchDes, 0.35);
        }
        pitchDes = clampValue(pitchDes, -0.5 * kPi + 0.01, 0.5 * kPi - 0.01);

        double pitchErr = wrapAngle(pitchDes - s.pitch);
        double yawErr = wrapAngle(yawDes - s.yaw);
        double rollErr = wrapAngle(-s.roll);

        double pitchCmd = clampValue(1.5 * pitchErr - 0.35 * s.pitchRate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double yawCmd = clampValue(1.5 * yawErr - 0.35 * s.yawRate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double rollCmd = clampValue(0.8 * rollErr - 0.30 * s.rollRate, -1.0, 1.0);

        Command out;
        out.thrust = thrust;
        out.pitchCmd = pitchCmd;
        out.yawCmd = yawCmd;
        out.rollCmd = rollCmd;

        if (!initialized_) {
            initialized_ = true;
            prev_ = out;
            return out;
        }

        // Mild smoothing to reduce control jitter while keeping responsiveness.
        out.thrust = 0.60 * prev_.thrust + 0.40 * out.thrust;
        out.pitchCmd = 0.50 * prev_.pitchCmd + 0.50 * out.pitchCmd;
        out.yawCmd = 0.50 * prev_.yawCmd + 0.50 * out.yawCmd;
        out.rollCmd = 0.50 * prev_.rollCmd + 0.50 * out.rollCmd;

        prev_ = out;
        return out;
    }

private:
    World world_;
    bool initialized_ = false;
    Command prev_;
};

void parseTerrainSummary(
    const std::string& terrainPath,
    int64_t width,
    int64_t depth,
    int targetX,
    int targetY,
    TerrainSummary* summary) {

    std::ifstream in(terrainPath, std::ios::binary);
    if (!in) {
        summary->ready.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
        return;
    }

    const int64_t targetIndex = (targetX >= 0 && targetY >= 0)
        ? static_cast<int64_t>(targetX) * depth + targetY
        : -1;

    const size_t kChunkSize = 6u * 1u * 1024u * 1024u; // 6MB (multiple of record size)
    std::vector<unsigned char> buffer(kChunkSize + 6u);

    size_t carry = 0;
    int64_t idx = 0;
    uint16_t maxTop = 0;

    while (true) {
        in.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(buffer.data() + carry), static_cast<std::streamsize>(kChunkSize));
        const size_t n = static_cast<size_t>(in.gcount());

        if (n == 0 && carry == 0) break;

        const size_t total = carry + n;
        const size_t usable = (total / 6u) * 6u;

        const unsigned char* p = buffer.data();
        for (size_t i = 0; i < usable; i += 6u) {
            const uint16_t a = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 0] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 1]) << 8));
            const uint16_t b = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 2] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 3]) << 8));
            const uint16_t c = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 4] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 5]) << 8));

            const uint16_t top = (c > b) ? c : a;
            if (top > maxTop) maxTop = top;

            if (idx == targetIndex) {
                summary->targetA.store(static_cast<double>(a), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetB.store(static_cast<double>(b), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetC.store(static_cast<double>(c), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetFound.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
            }

            ++idx;
        }

        carry = total - usable;
        if (carry > 0) {
            std::memmove(buffer.data(), buffer.data() + usable, carry);
        }

        if (n == 0) break;
    }

    (void)width;
    summary->maxTop.store(static_cast<double>(maxTop), std::memory_order_relaxed);
    summary->ready.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
}

} // namespace

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    World world;

    if (!(std::cin >> world.width >> world.depth >> world.height >> world.gravity >> world.maxThrust >> world.fuel0)) {
        return 0;
    }

    if (!(std::cin >> world.start.x >> world.start.y >> world.start.z
          >> world.target.x >> world.target.y >> world.target.z)) {
        return 0;
    }

    world.landingRadius = std::max(5.0, std::min(world.width, world.depth) * 0.02);

    std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n');
    std::string terrainPath;
    if (!std::getline(std::cin, terrainPath)) {
        return 0;
    }

    int64_t widthI = static_cast<int64_t>(std::llround(world.width));
    int64_t depthI = static_cast<int64_t>(std::llround(world.depth));

    int targetX = -1;
    int targetY = -1;
    if (widthI > 0 && depthI > 0) {
        targetX = static_cast<int>(clampValue<int64_t>(static_cast<int64_t>(std::floor(world.target.x)), 0, widthI - 1));
        targetY = static_cast<int>(clampValue<int64_t>(static_cast<int64_t>(std::floor(world.target.y)), 0, depthI - 1));
    }

    TerrainSummary terrain;
    std::thread terrainThread(parseTerrainSummary, terrainPath, widthI, depthI, targetX, targetY, &terrain);
    terrainThread.detach();

    std::mutex stateMutex;
    State latestState;
    bool hasState = false;

    std::atomic<bool> inputEnded{false};

    std::thread reader([&]() {
        uint64_t seq = 0;

        for (;;) {
            std::string first;
            if (!(std::cin >> first)) break;

            if (first == "STATE") {
                continue;
            }

            char* endPtr = nullptr;
            const double x = std::strtod(first.c_str(), &endPtr);
            if (endPtr == first.c_str() || *endPtr != '\0') {
                continue;
            }

            State s;
            s.x = x;

            if (!(std::cin >> s.y >> s.z
                  >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                  >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                  >> s.pitchRate >> s.yawRate >> s.rollRate
                  >> s.fuel)) {
                break;
            }

            s.seq = ++seq;
            s.stamp = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();

            {
                std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(stateMutex);
                latestState = s;
                hasState = true;
            }
        }

        inputEnded.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
    });

    Controller controller(world);

    std::cout << std::fixed << std::setprecision(6);

    auto nextTick = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
    const auto tick = std::chrono::milliseconds(40); // 25 Hz

    std::chrono::steady_clock::time_point lastStateStamp = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();

    for (;;) {
        nextTick += tick;

        State s;
        bool gotState = false;
        {
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(stateMutex);
            if (hasState) {
                s = latestState;
                gotState = true;
                lastStateStamp = s.stamp;
            }
        }

        Command cmd = gotState ? controller.compute(s, terrain) : controller.hoverCommand();

        cmd.thrust = clampValue(cmd.thrust, 0.0, 1.0);
        cmd.pitchCmd = clampValue(cmd.pitchCmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        cmd.yawCmd = clampValue(cmd.yawCmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        cmd.rollCmd = clampValue(cmd.rollCmd, -1.0, 1.0);

        std::cout << cmd.thrust << ' ' << cmd.pitchCmd << ' ' << cmd.yawCmd << ' ' << cmd.rollCmd << '\n';
        std::cout.flush();

        const auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
        const bool ended = inputEnded.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
        if (ended && (!gotState || now - lastStateStamp > std::chrono::milliseconds(700))) {
            break;
        }

        std::this_thread::sleep_until(nextTick);
    }

    if (reader.joinable()) {
        reader.join();
    }

    return 0;
}

View C++ Code (14399 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <atomic>
#include <chrono>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <fstream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <mutex>
#include <string>
#include <thread>
#include <vector>

namespace {

constexpr double kPi = 3.14159265358979323846264338327950288;

template <typename T>
T clampValue(T v, T lo, T hi) {
    return std::max(lo, std::min(hi, v));
}

double wrapAngle(double a) {
    while (a > kPi) a -= 2.0 * kPi;
    while (a < -kPi) a += 2.0 * kPi;
    return a;
}

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0.0;
    double y = 0.0;
    double z = 0.0;

    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double x_, double y_, double z_) : x(x_), y(y_), z(z_) {}

    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3(x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z); }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3(x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z); }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return Vec3(x * s, y * s, z * s); }
    Vec3 operator/(double s) const { return Vec3(x / s, y / s, z / s); }
};

double norm2D(double x, double y) {
    return std::sqrt(x * x + y * y);
}

double norm3D(const Vec3& v) {
    return std::sqrt(v.x * v.x + v.y * v.y + v.z * v.z);
}

struct World {
    double width = 0.0;
    double depth = 0.0;
    double height = 0.0;
    double gravity = 1.62;
    double maxThrust = 0.0;
    double fuel0 = 0.0;

    Vec3 start;
    Vec3 target;

    double landingRadius = 5.0;
};

struct State {
    double x = 0.0;
    double y = 0.0;
    double z = 0.0;

    double vx = 0.0;
    double vy = 0.0;
    double vz = 0.0;

    double pitch = 0.0;
    double yaw = 0.0;
    double roll = 0.0;

    double pitchRate = 0.0;
    double yawRate = 0.0;
    double rollRate = 0.0;

    double fuel = 0.0;

    uint64_t seq = 0;
    std::chrono::steady_clock::time_point stamp;
};

struct Command {
    double thrust = 0.0;
    double pitchCmd = 0.0;
    double yawCmd = 0.0;
    double rollCmd = 0.0;
};

struct TerrainSummary {
    std::atomic<bool> ready{false};
    std::atomic<double> maxTop{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetA{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetB{0.0};
    std::atomic<double> targetC{0.0};
    std::atomic<bool> targetFound{false};
};

class Controller {
public:
    explicit Controller(const World& w)
        : world_(w) {
        prev_.thrust = clampValue(world_.gravity / std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust), 0.0, 1.0);
    }

    Command hoverCommand() const {
        Command c;
        c.thrust = clampValue(world_.gravity / std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust), 0.0, 1.0);
        c.pitchCmd = 0.0;
        c.yawCmd = 0.0;
        c.rollCmd = 0.0;
        return c;
    }

    Command compute(const State& s, const TerrainSummary& terrain) {
        const double g = world_.gravity;
        const double maxT = std::max(1e-6, world_.maxThrust);

        const double dx = world_.target.x - s.x;
        const double dy = world_.target.y - s.y;
        const double dxy = norm2D(dx, dy);
        const double alt = s.z - world_.target.z;

        const double maxTop = terrain.maxTop.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
        const bool terrainReady = terrain.ready.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);

        const double safeMargin = std::max(8.0, 0.02 * world_.height);
        double safeZ = std::max({world_.start.z + 20.0, world_.target.z + 20.0, maxTop + safeMargin});
        safeZ = clampValue(safeZ, world_.target.z + 5.0, world_.height - 2.0);

        const double R = world_.landingRadius;

        double zRef;
        if (!terrainReady) {
            double provisional = std::max(world_.start.z, world_.target.z) + std::max(20.0, 0.1 * world_.height);
            zRef = clampValue(provisional, world_.target.z + 5.0, world_.height - 2.0);
        } else if (dxy > 6.0 * R) {
            zRef = safeZ;
        } else if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            zRef = clampValue(world_.target.z + std::max(6.0, 0.40 * dxy), world_.target.z + 2.0, safeZ);
        } else {
            zRef = world_.target.z + 0.6;
        }

        const double dirNorm = std::max(1e-9, dxy);
        const double dirX = dx / dirNorm;
        const double dirY = dy / dirNorm;

        double brakeA = std::max(0.9, 0.25 * (maxT - g));
        if (brakeA < 0.9) brakeA = 0.9;

        double speedCap;
        if (dxy > 6.0 * R) {
            speedCap = clampValue(0.06 * std::min(world_.width, world_.depth), 10.0, 120.0);
        } else if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            speedCap = 20.0;
        } else {
            speedCap = 4.0;
        }

        double vRefXY = std::min(speedCap, std::sqrt(std::max(0.0, 2.0 * brakeA * std::max(0.0, dxy - R))));
        if (dxy < 0.5 * R) vRefXY = std::min(vRefXY, 1.0);

        double vRefX = dirX * vRefXY;
        double vRefY = dirY * vRefXY;

        double vRefZ;
        if (dxy > 2.0 * R) {
            vRefZ = clampValue((zRef - s.z) * 0.60, -8.0, 8.0);
        } else {
            double desiredDown = clampValue(0.25 + 0.12 * std::max(0.0, alt), 0.2, 2.0);
            if (dxy > 0.8 * R) desiredDown = std::min(desiredDown, 0.8);
            if (alt < 1.2) desiredDown = std::min(desiredDown, 0.35);
            vRefZ = (alt > 0.0) ? -desiredDown : 0.4;
            if (norm2D(s.vx, s.vy) > 3.0) vRefZ = std::max(vRefZ, -0.3);
        }

        double kPxy = (dxy > 2.0 * R) ? 0.015 : 0.050;
        double kVxy = (dxy > 2.0 * R) ? 0.80 : 1.20;

        double ax = kVxy * (vRefX - s.vx) + kPxy * dx;
        double ay = kVxy * (vRefY - s.vy) + kPxy * dy;

        double kPz = 0.09;
        double kVz = 1.40;
        double az = kVz * (vRefZ - s.vz) + kPz * (zRef - s.z);

        double aXYLim = std::max(0.8, 0.55 * maxT);
        if (alt < 10.0) aXYLim *= 0.5;

        const double axy = norm2D(ax, ay);
        if (axy > aXYLim && axy > 1e-9) {
            const double sxy = aXYLim / axy;
            ax *= sxy;
            ay *= sxy;
        }

        const double azUpLim = std::max(0.4, maxT - g - 0.2);
        az = clampValue(az, -0.95 * g, azUpLim);

        if (alt < 2.0) {
            double neededUp = (-s.vz - 0.6) * 1.2;
            if (neededUp > az) az = neededUp;
            az = clampValue(az, -0.40 * g, azUpLim);
        }

        Vec3 thrustVec(ax, ay, az + g);
        if (thrustVec.z < 0.05) thrustVec.z = 0.05;

        double thrustMag = norm3D(thrustVec);
        const double maxUsableThrust = 0.98 * maxT;
        if (thrustMag > maxUsableThrust && thrustMag > 1e-9) {
            const double scale = maxUsableThrust / thrustMag;
            thrustVec = thrustVec * scale;
            thrustMag = maxUsableThrust;
        }

        double thrust = clampValue(thrustMag / maxT, 0.0, 1.0);

        const double h = norm2D(thrustVec.x, thrustVec.y);
        double pitchDes = std::atan2(h, std::max(1e-6, thrustVec.z));
        double yawDes = (h > 1e-9) ? std::atan2(-thrustVec.y, -thrustVec.x) : s.yaw;

        if (dxy < R && alt < 5.0) {
            pitchDes = std::min(pitchDes, 0.35);
        }
        pitchDes = clampValue(pitchDes, -0.5 * kPi + 0.01, 0.5 * kPi - 0.01);

        double pitchErr = wrapAngle(pitchDes - s.pitch);
        double yawErr = wrapAngle(yawDes - s.yaw);
        double rollErr = wrapAngle(-s.roll);

        double pitchCmd = clampValue(1.5 * pitchErr - 0.35 * s.pitchRate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double yawCmd = clampValue(1.5 * yawErr - 0.35 * s.yawRate, -1.0, 1.0);
        double rollCmd = clampValue(0.8 * rollErr - 0.30 * s.rollRate, -1.0, 1.0);

        Command out;
        out.thrust = thrust;
        out.pitchCmd = pitchCmd;
        out.yawCmd = yawCmd;
        out.rollCmd = rollCmd;

        if (!initialized_) {
            initialized_ = true;
            prev_ = out;
            return out;
        }

        // Mild smoothing to reduce control jitter while keeping responsiveness.
        out.thrust = 0.60 * prev_.thrust + 0.40 * out.thrust;
        out.pitchCmd = 0.50 * prev_.pitchCmd + 0.50 * out.pitchCmd;
        out.yawCmd = 0.50 * prev_.yawCmd + 0.50 * out.yawCmd;
        out.rollCmd = 0.50 * prev_.rollCmd + 0.50 * out.rollCmd;

        prev_ = out;
        return out;
    }

private:
    World world_;
    bool initialized_ = false;
    Command prev_;
};

void parseTerrainSummary(
    const std::string& terrainPath,
    int64_t width,
    int64_t depth,
    int targetX,
    int targetY,
    TerrainSummary* summary) {

    std::ifstream in(terrainPath, std::ios::binary);
    if (!in) {
        summary->ready.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
        return;
    }

    const int64_t targetIndex = (targetX >= 0 && targetY >= 0)
        ? static_cast<int64_t>(targetX) * depth + targetY
        : -1;

    const size_t kChunkSize = 6u * 1u * 1024u * 1024u; // 6MB (multiple of record size)
    std::vector<unsigned char> buffer(kChunkSize + 6u);

    size_t carry = 0;
    int64_t idx = 0;
    uint16_t maxTop = 0;

    while (true) {
        in.read(reinterpret_cast<char*>(buffer.data() + carry), static_cast<std::streamsize>(kChunkSize));
        const size_t n = static_cast<size_t>(in.gcount());

        if (n == 0 && carry == 0) break;

        const size_t total = carry + n;
        const size_t usable = (total / 6u) * 6u;

        const unsigned char* p = buffer.data();
        for (size_t i = 0; i < usable; i += 6u) {
            const uint16_t a = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 0] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 1]) << 8));
            const uint16_t b = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 2] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 3]) << 8));
            const uint16_t c = static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 4] | (static_cast<uint16_t>(p[i + 5]) << 8));

            const uint16_t top = (c > b) ? c : a;
            if (top > maxTop) maxTop = top;

            if (idx == targetIndex) {
                summary->targetA.store(static_cast<double>(a), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetB.store(static_cast<double>(b), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetC.store(static_cast<double>(c), std::memory_order_relaxed);
                summary->targetFound.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
            }

            ++idx;
        }

        carry = total - usable;
        if (carry > 0) {
            std::memmove(buffer.data(), buffer.data() + usable, carry);
        }

        if (n == 0) break;
    }

    (void)width;
    summary->maxTop.store(static_cast<double>(maxTop), std::memory_order_relaxed);
    summary->ready.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
}

} // namespace

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    World world;

    if (!(std::cin >> world.width >> world.depth >> world.height >> world.gravity >> world.maxThrust >> world.fuel0)) {
        return 0;
    }

    if (!(std::cin >> world.start.x >> world.start.y >> world.start.z
          >> world.target.x >> world.target.y >> world.target.z)) {
        return 0;
    }

    world.landingRadius = std::max(5.0, std::min(world.width, world.depth) * 0.02);

    std::cin.ignore(std::numeric_limits<std::streamsize>::max(), '\n');
    std::string terrainPath;
    if (!std::getline(std::cin, terrainPath)) {
        return 0;
    }

    int64_t widthI = static_cast<int64_t>(std::llround(world.width));
    int64_t depthI = static_cast<int64_t>(std::llround(world.depth));

    int targetX = -1;
    int targetY = -1;
    if (widthI > 0 && depthI > 0) {
        targetX = static_cast<int>(clampValue<int64_t>(static_cast<int64_t>(std::floor(world.target.x)), 0, widthI - 1));
        targetY = static_cast<int>(clampValue<int64_t>(static_cast<int64_t>(std::floor(world.target.y)), 0, depthI - 1));
    }

    TerrainSummary terrain;
    std::thread terrainThread(parseTerrainSummary, terrainPath, widthI, depthI, targetX, targetY, &terrain);
    terrainThread.detach();

    std::mutex stateMutex;
    State latestState;
    bool hasState = false;

    std::atomic<bool> inputEnded{false};

    std::thread reader([&]() {
        uint64_t seq = 0;

        for (;;) {
            std::string first;
            if (!(std::cin >> first)) break;

            if (first == "STATE") {
                continue;
            }

            char* endPtr = nullptr;
            const double x = std::strtod(first.c_str(), &endPtr);
            if (endPtr == first.c_str() || *endPtr != '\0') {
                continue;
            }

            State s;
            s.x = x;

            if (!(std::cin >> s.y >> s.z
                  >> s.vx >> s.vy >> s.vz
                  >> s.pitch >> s.yaw >> s.roll
                  >> s.pitchRate >> s.yawRate >> s.rollRate
                  >> s.fuel)) {
                break;
            }

            s.seq = ++seq;
            s.stamp = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();

            {
                std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(stateMutex);
                latestState = s;
                hasState = true;
            }
        }

        inputEnded.store(true, std::memory_order_relaxed);
    });

    Controller controller(world);

    std::cout << std::fixed << std::setprecision(6);

    auto nextTick = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
    const auto tick = std::chrono::milliseconds(40); // 25 Hz

    std::chrono::steady_clock::time_point lastStateStamp = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();

    for (;;) {
        nextTick += tick;

        State s;
        bool gotState = false;
        {
            std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(stateMutex);
            if (hasState) {
                s = latestState;
                gotState = true;
                lastStateStamp = s.stamp;
            }
        }

        Command cmd = gotState ? controller.compute(s, terrain) : controller.hoverCommand();

        cmd.thrust = clampValue(cmd.thrust, 0.0, 1.0);
        cmd.pitchCmd = clampValue(cmd.pitchCmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        cmd.yawCmd = clampValue(cmd.yawCmd, -1.0, 1.0);
        cmd.rollCmd = clampValue(cmd.rollCmd, -1.0, 1.0);

        std::cout << cmd.thrust << ' ' << cmd.pitchCmd << ' ' << cmd.yawCmd << ' ' << cmd.rollCmd << '\n';
        std::cout.flush();

        const auto now = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
        const bool ended = inputEnded.load(std::memory_order_relaxed);
        if (ended && (!gotState || now - lastStateStamp > std::chrono::milliseconds(700))) {
            break;
        }

        std::this_thread::sleep_until(nextTick);
    }

    if (reader.joinable()) {
        reader.join();
    }

    return 0;
}

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Launch an interceptor spacecraft to collide with a dangerous asteroid before it reaches Earth. The AI must plan a trajectory through the solar system, accounting for the gravitational pull of the Sun and planets, to hit a moving target millions of kilometres away.

Harder subpasses give less warning time or tighter fuel budgets, potentially requiring planetary gravity assists (slingshots) to reach the asteroid. The AI must implement orbital mechanics and trajectory optimisation from scratch.

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 4.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a cloud of 3D points, group them into clusters so that points in the same cluster are close together and points in different clusters are far apart. Think of sorting a jar of mixed beads by colour when all you can see is their position.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

 1.0000
[10K points, 10 clusters] Clusters used: 10/10, WCSS: 1.21e+09, Time: 0.17s

3D Point Clustering - 10K points, 10 clusters

View Rust Code (18235 chars)
use std::collections::HashSet;
use std::io::{self, BufRead, BufReader, BufWriter, Read, Write};

const SMALL_DATASET_MAX: u64 = 10_000_000;
const MEDIUM_DATASET_MAX: u64 = 1_000_000_000;

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
struct Point3 {
    x: f32,
    y: f32,
    z: f32,
}

impl Point3 {
    #[inline]
    fn dist2(self, other: Point3) -> f32 {
        let dx = self.x - other.x;
        let dy = self.y - other.y;
        let dz = self.z - other.z;
        dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz
    }

    #[inline]
    fn add_assign(&mut self, other: Point3) {
        self.x += other.x;
        self.y += other.y;
        self.z += other.z;
    }

    #[inline]
    fn scale(self, inv: f32) -> Point3 {
        Point3 {
            x: self.x * inv,
            y: self.y * inv,
            z: self.z * inv,
        }
    }
}

struct SplitMix64 {
    state: u64,
}

impl SplitMix64 {
    fn new(seed: u64) -> Self {
        Self { state: seed }
    }

    #[inline]
    fn next_u64(&mut self) -> u64 {
        self.state = self.state.wrapping_add(0x9E3779B97F4A7C15);
        let mut z = self.state;
        z = (z ^ (z >> 30)).wrapping_mul(0xBF58476D1CE4E5B9);
        z = (z ^ (z >> 27)).wrapping_mul(0x94D049BB133111EB);
        z ^ (z >> 31)
    }

    #[inline]
    fn gen_usize(&mut self, upper: usize) -> usize {
        if upper <= 1 {
            return 0;
        }
        ((self.next_u64() as u128 * upper as u128) >> 64) as usize
    }

    #[inline]
    fn gen_f64(&mut self) -> f64 {
        (self.next_u64() as f64) / (u64::MAX as f64)
    }
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
enum Strategy {
    InMemory,
    StreamingMiniBatch,
    StreamingExtreme,
}

struct CoarseIndex {
    macro_centers: Vec<Point3>,
    buckets: Vec<Vec<usize>>,
}

impl CoarseIndex {
    fn build(centers: &[Point3]) -> Self {
        let k = centers.len();
        if k == 0 {
            return Self {
                macro_centers: Vec::new(),
                buckets: Vec::new(),
            };
        }
        if k == 1 {
            return Self {
                macro_centers: vec![centers[0]],
                buckets: vec![vec![0]],
            };
        }

        let macro_count = ((k as f64).sqrt().round() as usize).clamp(1, 256).min(k);
        let mut macro_centers = Vec::with_capacity(macro_count);
        for i in 0..macro_count {
            macro_centers.push(centers[(i * k) / macro_count]);
        }

        let mut assignments = vec![0usize; k];

        for _ in 0..2 {
            let mut sums = vec![Point3::default(); macro_count];
            let mut counts = vec![0u32; macro_count];

            for (ci, &c) in centers.iter().enumerate() {
                let m = nearest_in_slice(c, &macro_centers);
                assignments[ci] = m;
                sums[m].add_assign(c);
                counts[m] += 1;
            }

            for m in 0..macro_count {
                if counts[m] > 0 {
                    macro_centers[m] = sums[m].scale(1.0 / counts[m] as f32);
                }
            }
        }

        let mut buckets = vec![Vec::<usize>::new(); macro_count];
        for (ci, &m) in assignments.iter().enumerate() {
            buckets[m].push(ci);
        }

        for m in 0..macro_count {
            if buckets[m].is_empty() {
                buckets[m].push((m * k) / macro_count);
            }
        }

        Self {
            macro_centers,
            buckets,
        }
    }
}

fn main() -> io::Result<()> {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let stdout = io::stdout();

    let mut reader = BufReader::with_capacity(8 * 1024 * 1024, stdin.lock());
    let mut writer = BufWriter::with_capacity(8 * 1024 * 1024, stdout.lock());

    let (num_points, num_clusters) = read_header(&mut reader)?;

    if num_clusters == 0 || num_points == 0 {
        writer.flush()?;
        return Ok(());
    }

    if num_clusters > u32::MAX as usize {
        return Err(io::Error::new(
            io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
            "num_clusters must fit into u32",
        ));
    }

    let strategy = choose_strategy(num_points);

    match strategy {
        Strategy::InMemory => run_in_memory(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, num_clusters)?,
        Strategy::StreamingMiniBatch => {
            run_streaming(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, num_clusters, false)?
        }
        Strategy::StreamingExtreme => {
            run_streaming(&mut reader, &mut writer, num_points, num_clusters, true)?
        }
    }

    writer.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

fn choose_strategy(num_points: u64) -> Strategy {
    if num_points <= SMALL_DATASET_MAX {
        Strategy::InMemory
    } else if num_points <= MEDIUM_DATASET_MAX {
        Strategy::StreamingMiniBatch
    } else {
        Strategy::StreamingExtreme
    }
}

fn read_header<R: BufRead>(reader: &mut R) -> io::Result<(u64, usize)> {
    let mut header = String::new();
    let bytes = reader.read_line(&mut header)?;
    if bytes == 0 {
        return Err(io::Error::new(
            io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof,
            "missing header line",
        ));
    }

    let mut it = header.split_whitespace();
    let num_points = it
        .next()
        .ok_or_else(|| io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, "missing num_points"))?
        .parse::<u64>()
        .map_err(|e| io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, format!("bad num_points: {e}")))?;

    let num_clusters = it
        .next()
        .ok_or_else(|| io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, "missing num_clusters"))?
        .parse::<usize>()
        .map_err(|e| io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::InvalidData, format!("bad num_clusters: {e}")))?;

    Ok((num_points, num_clusters))
}

fn read_point<R: Read>(reader: &mut R, buf: &mut [u8; 12]) -> io::Result<Option<Point3>> {
    let mut read = 0usize;
    while read < 12 {
        let n = reader.read(&mut buf[read..])?;
        if n == 0 {
            if read == 0 {
                return Ok(None);
            }
            return Err(io::Error::new(
                io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof,
                "truncated point data",
            ));
        }
        read += n;
    }

    let x = f32::from_le_bytes(buf[0..4].try_into().unwrap());
    let y = f32::from_le_bytes(buf[4..8].try_into().unwrap());
    let z = f32::from_le_bytes(buf[8..12].try_into().unwrap());

    Ok(Some(Point3 { x, y, z }))
}

fn write_u32<W: Write>(writer: &mut W, value: usize) -> io::Result<()> {
    writer.write_all(&(value as u32).to_le_bytes())
}

fn run_in_memory<R: Read, W: Write>(
    reader: &mut BufReader<R>,
    writer: &mut BufWriter<W>,
    num_points: u64,
    num_clusters: usize,
) -> io::Result<()> {
    let n = usize::try_from(num_points).map_err(|_| {
        io::Error::new(
            io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
            "num_points too large for this platform",
        )
    })?;

    let mut points = Vec::with_capacity(n);
    let mut buf = [0u8; 12];

    for _ in 0..n {
        match read_point(reader, &mut buf)? {
            Some(p) => points.push(p),
            None => {
                return Err(io::Error::new(
                    io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof,
                    "input ended before all points were read",
                ));
            }
        }
    }

    let mut rng = SplitMix64::new(seed_from(num_points, num_clusters as u64));
    let mut centers = init_centers(&points, num_clusters, &mut rng);

    let max_iters = if n <= 1_000_000 {
        25
    } else if num_clusters <= 512 {
        15
    } else {
        10
    };

    let assignments = lloyd_kmeans(&points, &mut centers, max_iters, 1e-4, &mut rng);

    for idx in assignments {
        write_u32(writer, idx)?;
    }

    Ok(())
}

fn run_streaming<R: Read, W: Write>(
    reader: &mut BufReader<R>,
    writer: &mut BufWriter<W>,
    num_points: u64,
    num_clusters: usize,
    extreme: bool,
) -> io::Result<()> {
    let warmup_size = compute_warmup_size(num_points, num_clusters, extreme);
    let warmup_u64 = warmup_size as u64;

    let mut sample = Vec::with_capacity(warmup_size);
    let mut buf = [0u8; 12];

    for _ in 0..warmup_u64.min(num_points) {
        match read_point(reader, &mut buf)? {
            Some(p) => sample.push(p),
            None => {
                return Err(io::Error::new(
                    io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof,
                    "input ended before warmup points were read",
                ));
            }
        }
    }

    if sample.is_empty() {
        return Ok(());
    }

    let mut rng = SplitMix64::new(seed_from(num_points ^ 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5, num_clusters as u64));
    let mut centers = init_centers(&sample, num_clusters, &mut rng);

    let refine_iters = if extreme { 4 } else { 8 };
    let batch_size = sample.len().min(if extreme { 120_000 } else { 400_000 });
    mini_batch_refine(&sample, &mut centers, refine_iters, batch_size, &mut rng);

    let mut counts = vec![0u64; num_clusters];
    let mut coarse = if num_clusters > 1024 {
        Some(CoarseIndex::build(&centers))
    } else {
        None
    };

    for &p in &sample {
        let (idx, _) = nearest_center(p, &centers, coarse.as_ref());
        counts[idx] += 1;
        online_update(&mut centers[idx], p, counts[idx], extreme);
        write_u32(writer, idx)?;
    }

    if num_clusters > 1024 {
        coarse = Some(CoarseIndex::build(&centers));
    }

    let mut produced = sample.len() as u64;
    let rebuild_every = if extreme { 5_000_000u64 } else { 1_000_000u64 };
    let mut since_rebuild = 0u64;

    while produced < num_points {
        let p = read_point(reader, &mut buf)?.ok_or_else(|| {
            io::Error::new(
                io::ErrorKind::UnexpectedEof,
                "input ended before all points were read",
            )
        })?;

        let (idx, _) = nearest_center(p, &centers, coarse.as_ref());
        counts[idx] += 1;
        online_update(&mut centers[idx], p, counts[idx], extreme);
        write_u32(writer, idx)?;

        produced += 1;
        since_rebuild += 1;

        if num_clusters > 1024 && since_rebuild >= rebuild_every {
            coarse = Some(CoarseIndex::build(&centers));
            since_rebuild = 0;
        }
    }

    Ok(())
}

fn compute_warmup_size(num_points: u64, num_clusters: usize, extreme: bool) -> usize {
    let max_points = usize::try_from(num_points).unwrap_or(usize::MAX);

    let mut target = num_clusters.saturating_mul(if extreme { 48 } else { 64 });
    target = target.max(if extreme { 50_000 } else { 200_000 });
    target = target.min(if extreme { 600_000 } else { 2_000_000 });

    let min_needed = num_clusters.min(max_points);
    target = target.max(min_needed);

    target.min(max_points)
}

fn init_centers(points: &[Point3], k: usize, rng: &mut SplitMix64) -> Vec<Point3> {
    if k == 0 {
        return Vec::new();
    }
    if points.is_empty() {
        return vec![Point3::default(); k];
    }

    let n = points.len();

    if k <= 128 && n <= 1_000_000 {
        return kmeans_plus_plus(points, k, rng);
    }

    let mut centers = Vec::with_capacity(k);

    if k <= n {
        let mut picked = HashSet::with_capacity(k.saturating_mul(2));
        while centers.len() < k {
            let idx = rng.gen_usize(n);
            if picked.insert(idx) {
                centers.push(points[idx]);
            }
        }
    } else {
        centers.extend_from_slice(points);
        while centers.len() < k {
            centers.push(points[rng.gen_usize(n)]);
        }
    }

    centers
}

fn kmeans_plus_plus(points: &[Point3], k: usize, rng: &mut SplitMix64) -> Vec<Point3> {
    if points.is_empty() || k == 0 {
        return Vec::new();
    }

    let n = points.len();
    let max_unique = k.min(n);

    let mut centers = Vec::with_capacity(k);
    centers.push(points[rng.gen_usize(n)]);

    let mut min_d2 = vec![f32::INFINITY; n];

    while centers.len() < max_unique {
        let last = *centers.last().unwrap();
        let mut total = 0.0f64;

        for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            let d = p.dist2(last);
            if d < min_d2[i] {
                min_d2[i] = d;
            }
            total += min_d2[i] as f64;
        }

        if total <= 0.0 {
            centers.push(points[rng.gen_usize(n)]);
            continue;
        }

        let mut target = rng.gen_f64() * total;
        let mut chosen = n - 1;

        for (i, &d) in min_d2.iter().enumerate() {
            target -= d as f64;
            if target <= 0.0 {
                chosen = i;
                break;
            }
        }

        centers.push(points[chosen]);
    }

    while centers.len() < k {
        centers.push(points[rng.gen_usize(n)]);
    }

    centers
}

fn lloyd_kmeans(
    points: &[Point3],
    centers: &mut [Point3],
    max_iters: usize,
    tolerance: f32,
    rng: &mut SplitMix64,
) -> Vec<usize> {
    let n = points.len();
    let k = centers.len();

    let mut assignments = vec![0usize; n];
    if n == 0 || k == 0 {
        return assignments;
    }

    let tol2 = tolerance * tolerance;

    for _ in 0..max_iters {
        let coarse = if k > 1024 {
            Some(CoarseIndex::build(centers))
        } else {
            None
        };

        let mut sums = vec![Point3::default(); k];
        let mut counts = vec![0u64; k];

        for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
            let (idx, _) = nearest_center(p, centers, coarse.as_ref());
            assignments[i] = idx;
            sums[idx].add_assign(p);
            counts[idx] += 1;
        }

        let mut max_shift = 0.0f32;

        for c in 0..k {
            let next = if counts[c] > 0 {
                sums[c].scale(1.0 / counts[c] as f32)
            } else {
                points[rng.gen_usize(n)]
            };

            let shift = centers[c].dist2(next);
            if shift > max_shift {
                max_shift = shift;
            }
            centers[c] = next;
        }

        if max_shift <= tol2 {
            break;
        }
    }

    let coarse = if k > 1024 {
        Some(CoarseIndex::build(centers))
    } else {
        None
    };

    for (i, &p) in points.iter().enumerate() {
        assignments[i] = nearest_center(p, centers, coarse.as_ref()).0;
    }

    assignments
}

fn mini_batch_refine(
    sample: &[Point3],
    centers: &mut [Point3],
    iters: usize,
    batch_size: usize,
    rng: &mut SplitMix64,
) {
    if sample.is_empty() || centers.is_empty() || batch_size == 0 || iters == 0 {
        return;
    }

    let k = centers.len();
    let mut counts = vec![0u64; k];

    for _ in 0..iters {
        let coarse = if k > 1024 {
            Some(CoarseIndex::build(centers))
        } else {
            None
        };

        for _ in 0..batch_size {
            let p = sample[rng.gen_usize(sample.len())];
            let (idx, _) = nearest_center(p, centers, coarse.as_ref());
            counts[idx] += 1;

            let seen = counts[idx];
            let cap = 4096u64;
            let eta = if seen < cap {
                1.0 / seen as f32
            } else {
                1.0 / cap as f32
            };

            centers[idx].x += (p.x - centers[idx].x) * eta;
            centers[idx].y += (p.y - centers[idx].y) * eta;
            centers[idx].z += (p.z - centers[idx].z) * eta;
        }
    }
}

#[inline]
fn online_update(center: &mut Point3, p: Point3, seen: u64, extreme: bool) {
    let cap = if extreme { 16384u64 } else { 4096u64 };
    let eta = if seen < cap {
        1.0 / seen as f32
    } else {
        1.0 / cap as f32
    };

    center.x += (p.x - center.x) * eta;
    center.y += (p.y - center.y) * eta;
    center.z += (p.z - center.z) * eta;
}

#[inline]
fn nearest_in_slice(p: Point3, centers: &[Point3]) -> usize {
    let mut best_idx = 0usize;
    let mut best_dist = f32::INFINITY;

    for (i, &c) in centers.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = p.dist2(c);
        if d < best_dist {
            best_dist = d;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }

    best_idx
}

#[inline]
fn nearest_two_macros(p: Point3, macros: &[Point3]) -> (usize, usize) {
    if macros.len() <= 1 {
        return (0, 0);
    }

    let mut best1 = 0usize;
    let mut best2 = 1usize;
    let mut d1 = p.dist2(macros[best1]);
    let mut d2 = p.dist2(macros[best2]);

    if d2 < d1 {
        std::mem::swap(&mut best1, &mut best2);
        std::mem::swap(&mut d1, &mut d2);
    }

    for (i, &m) in macros.iter().enumerate().skip(2) {
        let d = p.dist2(m);
        if d < d1 {
            best2 = best1;
            d2 = d1;
            best1 = i;
            d1 = d;
        } else if d < d2 {
            best2 = i;
            d2 = d;
        }
    }

    (best1, best2)
}

#[inline]
fn nearest_center(p: Point3, centers: &[Point3], coarse: Option<&CoarseIndex>) -> (usize, f32) {
    if let Some(index) = coarse {
        if !index.macro_centers.is_empty() {
            let (m1, m2) = nearest_two_macros(p, &index.macro_centers);
            let mut best_idx = 0usize;
            let mut best_dist = f32::INFINITY;

            for &ci in &index.buckets[m1] {
                let d = p.dist2(centers[ci]);
                if d < best_dist {
                    best_dist = d;
                    best_idx = ci;
                }
            }

            if m2 != m1 {
                for &ci in &index.buckets[m2] {
                    let d = p.dist2(centers[ci]);
                    if d < best_dist {
                        best_dist = d;
                        best_idx = ci;
                    }
                }
            }

            if best_dist.is_finite() {
                return (best_idx, best_dist);
            }
        }
    }

    let mut best_idx = 0usize;
    let mut best_dist = f32::INFINITY;

    for (i, &c) in centers.iter().enumerate() {
        let d = p.dist2(c);
        if d < best_dist {
            best_dist = d;
            best_idx = i;
        }
    }

    (best_idx, best_dist)
}

#[inline]
fn seed_from(a: u64, b: u64) -> u64 {
    let mut x = a ^ b.rotate_left(17) ^ 0xD6E8FEB86659FD93;
    x ^= x >> 32;
    x = x.wrapping_mul(0x9E3779B97F4A7C15);
    x ^= x >> 29;
    x
}
